tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

Co-authored-by: Arcturus Zhang <dapzhang@microsoft.com>","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-17T06:56:09Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-17T06:56:09Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Prepare release for Azure.ResourceManager.Dns Version 1.2.0-beta.3 (#58203)","author":{"name":"Arthur Ma","email":"mars.arthur@263.net","date":"2026-04-17T06:40:54Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-17T06:40:54Z"}},"author":{"login":"ArthurMa1978","id":20514459,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArthurMa1978","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20514459?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[mgmt] Fix null-safe flattened property getters (#58198)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Wei Hu","email":"live1206@gmail.com","date":"2026-04-17T05:11:42Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-17T05:11:42Z"}},"author":{"login":"live1206","id":5196139,"profile_url":"https://github.com/live1206","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Spec compliance, chat isolation, and observability (#58252)

* Eager eviction of completed ResponseExecution from tracker

Replace MarkCompleted (which kept completed executions in memory forever)
with TryEvict (which removes from the dictionary immediately after
finalization). This fixes a memory leak where completed executions
accumulated indefinitely in the ResponseExecutionTracker.

After eviction, all API calls (GET, DELETE, Cancel, SSE replay) for
completed responses fall through to the storage provider, which is the
correct source of truth for persisted data.

Key changes:
- ResponseExecutionTracker: replace MarkCompleted with TryEvict
- ResponseExecution: remove unused CompletedAt property
- ResponseOrchestrator: simplified guards (all tracked are in-flight)
- ResponseEndpointHandler: added provider-path SSE replay guard
- SseReplayResult: catch BadRequestException from stream provider
- InMemoryResponsesProvider: throw BadRequestException (not IOE) for
  missing event streams — gives honest error for both 'never had stream'
  and 'stream TTL expired' cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Align error shapes with container spec (TDD)

- Default code to 'invalid_request_error' in InvalidRequest() and NotFound()
- Cancel incomplete: 'Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.'
- SSE replay: split into distinct messages for background/stream with param
- Delete in-flight: 'Cannot delete an in-flight response.'
- Tests updated first (TDD red/green) across 8 test files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Spec compliance: post-delete 404, C6/usage/cancel-after-delete tests

Align with responses-api-behaviour-contract.md Post-Deletion Behaviour:
- GET/input_items/cancel after DELETE now return 404 (was 400)
- InMemoryResponsesProvider tombstone checks throw ResourceNotFoundException

New protocol tests closing audit gaps:
- C6 (store=false + stream=true + no-bg): SSE stream completes, GET 404
- Cancel after DELETE: returns 404 per spec
- Token usage B33: handler-provided ResponseUsage appears in JSON
  response body, SSE response.completed event, and GET on background
  responses; absent usage serializes as null

All 1872 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Integrate ID + metadata validation into validator pipeline (B40, B29)

- Fix spec typo: 'Malformed identitifer.' → 'Malformed identifier.'
- Move previous_response_id format validation into
  CreateResponsePayloadValidatorCustom (B29 pipeline, code: invalid_value,
  param: $.previous_response_id)
- Move metadata constraints (16 pairs, 64-char keys, 512-char values) into
  MetadataValidatorCustom (B29 pipeline)
- Remove duplicate handler-level ValidateMetadata() and previous_response_id
  checks from ResponseEndpointHandler
- Path-parameter B40 validation stays in handler (code: invalid_parameters,
  param: responseId{<value>})
- Update all tests using fake 'resp_*' or short 'caresp_*' IDs to use
  IdGenerator.NewResponseId() to avoid B40 rejections
- Add 3 new test files: MalformedIdProtocolTests (7 tests),
  MetadataConstraintProtocolTests (8 tests),
  CreationIdValidationProtocolTests (3 tests)

All 1890 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints

When a response is created with a chat isolation key (x-agent-chat-isolation-key),
all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must provide the same key.
Mismatch or missing key returns standard 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)
to prevent cross-chat information leakage. No enforcement when created without a key.

Enforcement points:
- In-flight (tracker): orchestrator GetAsync/CancelAsync, handler SSE GET/DELETE
- Persisted (provider): InMemoryResponsesProvider Get/Delete/InputItems

Implementation:
- ResponseExecution.ChatIsolationKey: stores creation-time key for in-flight checks
- EnforceInFlightChatIsolation(): shared helper in handler and orchestrator
- InMemoryResponsesProvider._chatIsolationKeys: ConcurrentDictionary for persisted checks

17 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints (match/mismatch/missing),
in-flight background responses, and error body verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add HttpClient instrumentation for outbound trace correlation

Register AddHttpClientInstrumentation() for both tracing and metrics in
the OTLP-only path (non-Azure Monitor). This exports outbound HTTP client
spans so distributed traces show the full call chain through the Foundry
storage layer. UseAzureMonitor already registers both instrumentations,
so the existing guard prevents duplicate spans.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update changelogs for Responses beta.2 and Core beta.22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add structured logging to FoundryStorageProvider

Use LoggerMessage source-generated methods to log every outbound Foundry
storage API call with operation name, response ID, HTTP status code, and
duration. Debug level for success, Warning for HTTP errors, Error for
exceptions. Never logs credentials or PII.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace inline logging with FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy

Move Foundry storage request/response logging from FoundryStorageProvider
into a dedicated HttpPipeline per-retry policy. This captures correlation
headers on both sides:
- Request: x-ms-client-request-id (stamped by Azure.Core)
- Response: x-ms-request-id (returned by Foundry service)

Each retry attempt is logged independently with method, URI, status code,
and duration. FoundryStorageProvider reverts to its clean form with no
logging dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log x-request-id and apim-request-id correlation headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Raise storage success log level to Information

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include URI in success and failure log messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy to Responses changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: centralize chat isolation, fix changelog, null guard

- Move EnforceInFlightChatIsolation to ResponseExecution.EnforceChatIsolation()
  instance method, removing duplication from handler and orchestrator
- Add null guard for execution.Response in GET path (pre-response.created race)
- Fix changelog: remove incorrect conversation_id claim, fix error type names
  (invalid_request_error not unsupported_parameter), fix cancel-after-delete (404
  not conflict_error), add missing Breaking Changes header
- Update SseReplayResult error message to cover both non-streaming and TTL-expired
  cases with TODO for spec-distinct messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

resource.text:
# Azure AI Agent Server libraries for .NET

The Azure AI Agent Server libraries let you build ASP.NET Core servers that implement Azure AI agent protocols. Deploy existing agents — whether built with supported agent frameworks or custom code — into Microsoft AI Foundry with minimal effort.

## Packages

| Package | Description | NuGet |
|---------|-------------|-------|
| [Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core) | Shared hosting foundation: library-owned ASP.NET Core host with OpenTelemetry, health checks, server user-agent header, and multi-protocol composition. | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/vpre/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core) |
| [Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses) | Responses protocol implementation: SSE streaming, background execution, response lifecycle management, and `ResponseHandler` interface. | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/vpre/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses) |
| [Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations) | Invocations protocol implementation: `InvocationHandler` abstract class, session resolution, client header forwarding, and invocation lifecycle. | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/vpre/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations) |

## When to use which package

- **Core** is the foundation — install it when you need a server host. It is automatically referenced by both protocol packages.
- **Responses** implements the Azure AI Responses API (SSE streaming, function calling, conversation history). Use it when your agent communicates via the Responses protocol.
- **Invocations** implements the Azure AI Invocations protocol (request/response handler pattern). Use it when your agent communicates via the Invocations protocol.
- **Both protocols together**: Use `AgentHostBuilder` with `.AddResponses<T>()` and `.AddInvocations<T>()` to compose both protocols on a single host.

## Getting started

### Responses protocol

The fastest way to get a Responses protocol server running:

```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConfigureServer_Tier1
ResponsesServer.Run<EchoHandler>();
```

Where `EchoHandler` implements the Responses protocol:

```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler
public class EchoHandler : ResponseHandler
{
    public override IAsyncEnumerable<ResponseStreamEvent> CreateAsync(
        CreateResponse request,
        ResponseContext context,
        CancellationToken cancellationToken)
    {
        return new TextResponse(context, request,
            createText: async ct =>
            {
                var input = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken: ct);
                return $"Echo: {input}";
            });
    }
}
```

### Invocations protocol

The fastest way to get an Invocations protocol server running:

```C# Snippet:Invocations_Sample1_StartServer
InvocationsServer.Run<EchoHandler>();
```

Where `EchoHandler` implements the Invocations protocol:

```C# Snippet:Invocations_Sample1_EchoHandler
public class EchoHandler : InvocationHandler
{
    public override async Task HandleAsync(
        HttpRequest request, HttpResponse response,
        InvocationContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
    {
        var input = await new StreamReader(request.Body).ReadToEndAsync(cancellationToken);
        await response.WriteAsync($"You said: {input}", cancellationToken);
    }
}
```

See each package's README for detailed getting started instructions.

## Contributing

See the [Azure SDK CONTRIBUTING.md][contrib] for details on building, testing, and contributing to this library.

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit [cla.microsoft.com][cla].

When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., label, comment). Simply follow the instructions provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][code_of_conduct]. For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][code_of_conduct_faq] or contact [opencode@microsoft.com][email_opencode] with any additional questions or comments.

<!-- LINKS -->
[contrib]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
[cla]: https://cla.microsoft.com
[code_of_conduct]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/
[code_of_conduct_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/
[email_opencode]: mailto:opencode@microsoft.com

# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.2 (2026-05-05)

### Features Added

- Added chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints. When a response is created with
  `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must
  include the same key. Mismatched or missing keys return 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)
  to ensure tenant isolation.
- Added validation for malformed response IDs in both the `response_id` path parameter and the
  `previous_response_id` request body field. IDs that do not match the expected format (prefix
  and length) are rejected with 400 and a descriptive error message.
- Added metadata constraint validation: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys with key length ≤ 64
  and value length ≤ 512 characters, enforced via the validator pipeline.
- Added deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation: SHA-256 hash of
  `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars.
  Falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available.
- Added eager eviction of completed `ResponseExecution` entries from the in-flight tracker,
  reducing memory pressure for long-running servers.
- Added `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` — an Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every
  outbound Foundry storage API call with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation
  headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).

### Bugs Fixed

- Fixed error response shapes to match the container specification: `invalid_request_error` type
  for 400 errors and `not_found` message format.
- Fixed cancel-after-terminal to return `invalid_request_error` type with the correct error shape
  per the specification.
- Fixed DELETE endpoint to return 404 (not 400) when the response ID does not exist, aligning
  with the specification.
- Fixed cancel-after-delete to return 404 (not-found) per the specification.

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-04-14)

### Features Added

- Initial release of the Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses library.
- ASP.NET Core server library implementing the Azure AI Responses API.
- `ResponseHandler` abstract class for custom response handling with `CreateAsync` returning `IAsyncEnumerable<ResponseStreamEvent>`.
- `TextResponse` convenience class for simple text-only responses with a single delegate.
- `ResponseEventStream` builder for full control over SSE event generation with automatic `sequenceNumber`, `outputIndex`, `contentIndex`, and `itemId` tracking.
- `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:
  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)
  - `OutputItemMessage(string text, IEnumerable<Annotation> annotations)` — emits a text message with file annotations
  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable<string> tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events
  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item
  - `OutputItemFunctionCallOutput(callId, output)` — emits a function call output (no deltas)
  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item
  - `OutputItemImageGenCall(resultBase64)` — emits an image generation result with status transitions
  - `OutputItemStructuredOutputs(output)` — emits an arbitrary structured JSON output item
  - One-liner convenience generators for all remaining simple output item types: computer calls, local shell calls, function shell calls, apply-patch calls, custom tool call outputs, MCP approval requests/responses, and compaction
- `ResponseContext` with `GetInputItemsAsync()` (returns `IReadOnlyList<Item>`) and `GetInputTextAsync()` convenience for accessing resolved input items and text content.
- `IEnumerable<Item>.GetInputText()` extension method for extracting text from any sequence of input items.
- `CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` extension for advanced access to expanded input items as `OutputItem` types.
- Built-in in-memory response provider and execution tracking.
- Support for default, streaming, background, and streaming+background response modes.
- `AgentHostBuilder` convenience methods for zero-config server startup via `ResponsesServer.Run<T>()`.
- Protocol identity registration with `ServerUserAgentRegistry` during route mapping.
- `x-agent-response-id` header validation matching the Responses API specification.
- Conversation ID round-trip support in both synchronous and SSE streaming modes.
- OpenTelemetry distributed tracing via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2: - **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation. - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). - **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars. - **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400. - **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently. ## Gaps Found - The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is. - The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only "The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source." It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics. - The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs. - No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit** #### 1a. Add a "Chat isolation" subsection under Key concepts After the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add: ```markdown ### Chat isolation The `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is created with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found` (indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible. When no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls. ``` #### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section Replace the existing bullet list with: ```markdown ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format. - **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id` request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters). - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state."`. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not match the key provided in this request. - **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec. ``` #### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section Replace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. #### Foundry storage call logging When the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`), `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and `Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes: - HTTP method and URI - HTTP status code and latency - Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry) To enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for `Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer": "Information" } } } ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are explanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is desired, add a new test file: **Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs` Wrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion` and place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README's new "Chat isolation" subsection. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no sections need to be created from scratch. Current structure (all present): 1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server 2. ✅ Key concepts — needs "Chat isolation" subsection added 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README
